title: Odontosoria alutacea
intro: Odontosoria alutacea, also known as the slender toothed fern, is a species of fern in the family Lindsaeaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Odontosoria alutacea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Odontosoria alutacea is commonly made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat a range of health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Odontosoria alutacea is classified as a species of fern in the genus Odontosoria, which is a part of the family Lindsaeaceae. This species belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which includes all types of ferns and their allies. The kingdom to which Odontosoria alutacea belongs is Plantae, encompassing all plant species. Within the classification hierarchy, Odontosoria alutacea is a member of the phylum Filicophyta. Its classification can be summarized as: Odontosoria alutacea, kingdom: Plantae, division: Pteridophyta, phylum: Filicophyta, family: Lindsaeaceae, genus: Odontosoria.
